description: TREG Calibration Dataset — Reference cases for epistemic pattern matching

categories.vindicated_after_suppression: Researchers/ideas suppressed by institutional consensus and later vindicated
categories.correctly_rejected: Claims that mainstream science correctly identified as wrong
categories.suppressed_ambiguous: Suppressed ideas with genuine but unresolved evidence
categories.institutional_capture_documented: Cases where institutional incentives distorted scientific consensus
categories.currently_contested: Active disputes where institutional dynamics may influence evaluation

cases.name: Semmelweis — Handwashing
cases.summary: Demonstrated handwashing reduced maternal mortality from 18% to 1%. Rejected by medical establishment. Died in asylum. Vindicated by germ theory.
cases.pattern: Empirical evidence dismissed because mechanism unknown. Institutional pride over patient outcomes.

cases.name: Marshall — H. pylori
cases.summary: Proved ulcers caused by bacteria, not stress. Self-experimented. Ridiculed for years. Nobel Prize 2005.
cases.pattern: Challenged profitable treatment paradigm. Self-experimentation as last resort.

cases.name: Wegener — Continental Drift
cases.summary: Proposed continental drift. Rejected 50 years. Vindicated by plate tectonics.
cases.pattern: Outsider to discipline. Evidence strong but mechanism unknown.

cases.name: Bretz — Channeled Scablands
cases.summary: Proposed catastrophic flooding carved Eastern Washington. Uniformitarianism orthodoxy resisted 40 years.
cases.pattern: Paradigm conflict. Field evidence clear but contradicted dominant philosophy.

cases.name: Nordenström — Bioelectric Medicine
cases.summary: Documented bioelectric circuits in the body. Published by major press but ignored by establishment.
cases.pattern: Credentialed researcher, published evidence, framework too revolutionary.

cases.name: N-rays
cases.summary: René Blondlot claimed N-rays. Replication failed. Exposed as observer bias by Robert Wood.
cases.pattern: Initial excitement, replication failure, decisive debunking experiment.

cases.name: Polywater
cases.summary: Claimed anomalous water form. Thousands of papers. Shown to be contaminated samples.
cases.pattern: Cascade of confirmation without adequate controls. Mundane explanation found.

cases.name: Lysenko — Vernalization
cases.summary: Rejected Mendelian genetics for ideological reasons. State enforcement. Set Soviet biology back decades.
cases.pattern: Political enforcement of incorrect science. No independent replication.

cases.name: Reich — Orgone/Biofields
cases.summary: Proposed orgone energy. FDA burned books. Early work credible, late work extraordinary. Modern biofield research echoes core claims.
cases.pattern: Credentialed early career, escalating claims, government material destruction, modern echoes.

cases.name: Rife — Frequency Therapy
cases.summary: Claimed frequency device destroyed pathogens. AMA opposition. Equipment confiscated. TTFields show frequency approaches have merit.
cases.pattern: Institutional opposition. Equipment destruction. Partial modern vindication via different mechanism.

cases.name: Tobacco — Cancer Denial
cases.summary: Funded doubt about smoking-cancer link for decades. Internal memos showed they knew.
cases.pattern: Manufactured doubt. Internal knowledge contradicting public position.

cases.name: Sugar Industry — Fat Blame
cases.summary: Funded Harvard researchers to blame fat not sugar for heart disease. Shaped dietary guidelines for decades.
cases.pattern: Funded research with predetermined conclusions. Shaped policy.

cases.name: Pharma Revolving Door
cases.summary: FDA officials move to industry positions. Approval incentives aligned with industry over patients.
cases.pattern: Structural incentive misalignment. Regulatory capture via career paths.

cases.name: UAP/Non-Human Intelligence
cases.summary: Decades of ridicule then 2017 NYT revelations, AARO, congressional hearings, PURSUE Act 2026.
cases.pattern: Institutional ridicule then forced disclosure. Signal quality improving.

cases.name: Consciousness as Fundamental
cases.summary: IIT, GWT, and alternatives suggest consciousness may be fundamental. HIRM proposes multiplicative integration.
cases.pattern: Paradigm challenge to physicalist assumptions. Mathematical frameworks exist.
